title_original: Pasqal Introduces New Integration with NVIDIA CUDA-Q to Enhance Its Hybrid Quantum Computing Environment for HPC - Pasqal
summary_original: Pasqal has announced the integration of the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform with its Quantum Resource Management Interface, allowing CUDA-Q workloads to run on Pasqal’s neutral-atom quantum systems within standard Slurm high-performance computing workflows. This development enables seamless scheduling of quantum processing units alongside classical processors and graphics processing units, aiming to reduce adoption barriers for enterprise and research computing teams. The integration, developed in collaboration with IBM, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, and STFC Hartree Centre, is already available via Pasqal’s cloud and will be deployed on CINECA’s Leonardo supercomputer. Pasqal, founded in 2019 and employing over 275 people, sees this as a step towards scalable, production-grade hybrid quantum-classical computing applications.
